revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:長福寺裏山古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Kasaoka|qid=Q863739|1=Kasaoka}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.58338888888889|N|133.52800555555555|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}
'''The Chofukuji Urayama Kofun Group is''' a group of ancient tombs located in Hashiride and Yamaguchi , Kasaoka City , Okayama Prefecture . It is designated as a Kasaoka City designated historic site. == overview == This is the largest group of ancient tombs in Kasaoka City, built on the hill ridge of the Oda River basin in western Okayama Prefecture . Eleven tombs have been confirmed to date. Excavations were carried out in 1961 ( Showa 36) and from 1997 to 2000 ( Heisei 9-12) . It is made up of kofun of various shapes, including keyhole-shaped kofun, scallop-shaped kofun (circular kofun with a projection), circular kofun, and square kofun, and is lined up from west to east with the Nanatsuzuka Tombs, Futatsuzuka Tombs, Hitotsuzuka Tombs, Senninzuka Tombs, and Higashizuka Tombs . [ 2 ] Although the Nanatsuzuka square kofun, the rare early sueki ware unearthed there suggests a connection with the Korean Peninsula. In addition, Futatsuzuka Tombs are the largest keyhole-shaped kofun in the western Bitchu region (Ikasa region), and Senninzuka Tombs have two pit-type stone chambers, one above the other, and the lower stone chamber is thought to be a pit-type horizontal-entrance stone chamber, a precursor to the horizontal-entrance stone chamber . All of these kofun are distinctive. The Chofukuji Urayama Kofun Group is estimated to have been built in the 5th century during the middle of the Kofun period , and is believed to have been built in the following order from west to east: Nanatsuzuka Tomb Group (first half of the 5th century), Futatsuzuka Tomb (mid-5th century), Senninzuka Tomb and Higashizuka Tomb (second half of the 5th century). Within Okayama Prefecture, the construction of huge mid-Kofun tombs is known in the Kibi region, but this group of tombs is considered important as it suggests the rapid rise of power in this region, which is somewhat removed from the center of Kibi .
{{Kofun navbar|qq=}}
{{translated page|ja|長福寺裏山古墳群|version=99995005|comment=Imported full ja history}}
